title: THE SEVENTH GOSWAMI | SRILA BHAKTIVINOD THAKUR APPEARANCE DAY SPECIAL | HG Vrajraj Das | HKM Mumbai
speaker: HG Amitasana Dasa

segments.text: Welcome to all the devotees for our Gita Live session.
And today is a very auspicious day.
Today is the appearance day of His Divine Grace Bhakti Vinod Thakur.
So today we will discuss about His glories and different activities He performed
by which we can learn a lot of lessons.
So before we start, we will offer some prayers.
So you can join your hands and just chant these prayers with me.
And then we will discuss about His Divine Grace Bhakti Vinod Thakur.
So you can chant with me prayers.
Shreemate Bhakti Vedanta Swamin Niti Naamine
Shri Advaita Gadadhara Shri Vasadi Gaur Bhakt Vrinda
Krishnaya Krishna Chetanya Namne Gaurat She Namaha
Namoh Brahmne Devaya Go Brahmne Taicha
Gopesha Gopika Kanta Radha Kanta Namostute
Vanchya Kalpatru Bhesha Kirpa Sindhu Bhewacha
Hare Krishna Hare Krishna Krishna Krishna Hare Hare
Hare Rama Hare Rama Rama Rama Hare Hare
So today is a very auspicious day,
a day when a very important acharya,
his divine grace Bhakti Vinod Thakur appeared on this planet earth.
So it is mentioned in the scriptures to give us real knowledge,
to give us real light in our life,
so that we can understand what is the purpose of our life,
why we are trapped in this cycle of birth and death,
As Krishna says in Bhagavad Gita that
Mavev Aam Sho Jeeva Loke Jeeva Bhuta Sanathana
All living entities are part and parcel of Krishna
and they are struggling in this material world with their mind and senses.
they are not able to understand their real position.
The position of this miserable material world,
where we are going through this repeated cycle of birth and death,
birth, death, old age and disease.
These are the miseries of this material world.
So Krishna comes here in this material world,
so that he can give us this real knowledge about our existence,
how we can become free from this cycle of repeated birth and death.
So as all of us know,
Krishna appeared on this planet earth 5-10 years back
and he gave this knowledge of Bhagavad Gita.
But even though Lord appears in this material world,
but with the passage of time,
he gives us this real spiritual knowledge,
but with the passage of time,
So Krishna established four authorised Guru Parampara,
through which this knowledge is getting passed on
to the suffering living entities in this material world.
Just like Krishna gave this knowledge,
first of all to Lord Brahma,
and like that this Parampara continues.

Yes, anybody can practice Bhakti. Just like we can see example of A.C. Bhaktivedanta Swami Srila Prabhupada.
So, when Prabhupada went to the western world, so we can all see that way back in 1965 when Srila Prabhupada went.
At that time, as all of us know, all the westerners were engaged in all kinds of sinful activities like meat-eating, gambling, illicit sex.
So, Prabhupada started this Hare Krishna movement in the west and in the midst of all these kinds of sinful people.
So, but yes, when we practice Bhakti, we can all see that way back in 1965 when Srila Prabhupada went to the western world.
So, when we practice the process of chanting Hare Krishna Maha Mantra and we follow all the instructions as given by Srila Prabhupada,
definitely, as Krishna says in Bhagavad Gita, when a person gets higher taste, automatically he will lose the lower taste.
So, the concept is that we have to take shelter of a bona fide guru and follow his instructions irrespective of whatever is...
So, when we follow instructions of a guru, then the process will work and definitely when we get this higher taste,
automatically we lose all kinds of other lower taste.
So, that is what happened in west.
Prabhupada went there and he started this movement in the midst of all these sinful people and then all of them, they became devotees.
They left all their bad habits.
So, when they can leave all their bad habits, why not others?
So, especially in Kalyuga, we see, even in India, we see a lot of people are non-vegetarian or they are addicted to some of the sinful activities because Kalyuga is like that.
So, there is no obstacle when it comes to Bhakti.
So, next question is by Sahil.
So, we have to understand what is the meaning of lust.
And because of this lust only, all of us are trapped in this cycle of repeated birth and death.
So, the goal of life is to transform.
Love means which exists between a Jeeva and Krishna.
Love can only exist between living entities and Krishna.
So, if we really want to come out of this lust, then we have to develop love for Krishna.
Lust means whatever activities you do.
That is for satisfying my senses, my mind.
Love means I will do all those activities which are meant for satisfying senses of Krishna.
Just like if suppose somebody is fond of eating.
So, he can continue eating, but he can bring this concept of love for Krishna.
We can take this determination that I will eat food only after offering to Krishna.
So, just by a small change.
That we are eating that food, but only after offering to Krishna.
So, if we bring like this, a small change in our activities, then that lust will slowly transform into love.

We can follow instructions of Srila Prabhupada like everyday chanting of Hare Krishna Maha Mantra and then reading of scriptures like that slowly, slowly if we follow the instructions and also keep this in the mind, back of the mind that I have to do all my activities for satisfying senses of Krishna, then this lust will get transformed into love for Krishna.
Hare Krishna Prabhuji, how to stay away from materialistic attractions in Grasth Ashrama?
So, the question is like how to stay away from Maya?
So, Srila Prabhupada says many places that actually there are only two things that is Krishna and Maya.
Maya means all this materialistic attractions.
So, the solution is if your mind is not engaged in serving Krishna or thinking of Krishna, then your mind will be forced to think about Maya or about this materialistic attractions.
So, if you really want to become free from all this materialistic attractions.
Actually, the simplest process is to become free from all these materialistic attractions.
So, if you really want to become free from all these materialistic attractions.
Actually, the simplest process is to always think of Krishna.
That is why it is mentioned in the scriptures that essence of all the scriptures is to always remember Krishna and never forget Krishna.
Now, question arises how we can remember always Krishna?
So, the simplest method of always remembering Krishna is to chant Hare Krishna Mahamand.

He was also a Krishna follower.
But now how can I get guidance to progress in spiritual life.
So the first thing is we have to see is that first of all Guru is not limited to physical
Just like when we accept Srila Prabhupada as our bona fide spiritual master.
So today itself a lot of devotees are taking Diksha or shelter of AC Bhaktivedanta Swamishila
Prabhupada although he may not be physically present.
So Guru means when we till the point his instructions are present in our life.
So in fact if you see our very famous video of very famous short film about empowered
At the end of that film one reporter actually asked Srila Prabhupada that what will happen
to this woman when you will die.
And Prabhupada mentioned that I will never die.
In fact Prabhupada says that I will live in my books.
So actually till the point instructions of Gurus Gurus are present till that point that
Guru is actually present with us.
Guru means who is not limited by his physical body.
So that is a proper understanding.
Another thing is we have to accept shelter of a Guru who is coming in a bona fide Sampradaya.
As I mentioned just like Bhaktivinoda Thakur is coming in a bona fide Sampradaya.
So we have to take this care that we accept shelter of a Guru who is a pure devotee and
is coming in a bona fide Sampradaya.
Only then actually that person can take us back to Godhead and actually that that person
